page: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical x86_64 cores using an Intel Core Processor from the Haswell architecture, clocked at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 60.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and processing speed. The CPU performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core results in the bottom 10% and multi-core results in the bottom 25%. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores also fall into the bottom 25%. However, memory performance is more balanced; the PassMark memory score and cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth of 339.78 GB/sec both rank in the middle 50%. Conversely, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth drops to the bottom 10% at 19.87 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is in the bottom 25% at 15.43 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r2-60 offers a high density of RAM relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intense CPU throughput. It is suited for workloads such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-intensive data processing where processing speed is secondary to memory capacity. It is less suitable for CPU-bound tasks, high-throughput web serving, or large language model inference, as demonstrated by its weak performance in Redis SET operations and poor 7B model text generation speeds.
description: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor clocked at 2.2 GHz, paired with 60.0 GB of RAM and a 100 GB local SSD. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant tradeoff: while the PassMark memory score and cached small-block read bandwidth perform at an average level, the CPU performance is weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25%. Uncached large-block memory bandwidth also shows limitations. Consequently, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands, such as caching layers, small databases, or basic data processing tasks, rather than compute-heavy applications or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 60 GB of RAM, and a 100 GB SSD, offering high memory density for workloads with moderate compute requirements.
meta_description: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized KVM server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 60 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining sixty gigabytes of RAM with dedicated compute cores for memory-intensive, low-CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs at 2.2 GHz
60 GB of RAM providing 15 GB per core
Average cached memory read bandwidth of 339.78 GB/sec
Best suited for caching layers and lightweight database operations
